Class LatencyTracker

java.lang.Object
org.efs.util.LatencyTracker
public final class LatencyTracker extends Object
Measures and tracks latency metrics for performance analysis of timed operations. Collects individual latency deltas (time measurements in nanoseconds), computes statistical metrics, and organizes deltas into configurable histogram buckets for detailed latency distribution analysis.

Purpose:

This class is designed for performance testing and benchmarking scenarios where detailed latency metrics are needed. It collects up to a configured maximum number of latency measurements and computes comprehensive statistics including minimum, maximum, average, median, and percentile latencies (95th, 99th, 99.9th, and 99.99th percentiles).

Latency Bucketing:

Collected latencies are automatically organized into histogram buckets to visualize the distribution of latency measurements. Each bucket tracks a configurable nanosecond time interval. An overflow bucket captures all measurements exceeding the maximum bucket threshold.

Configuration:

LatencyTracker instances are created using the LatencyTracker.Builder pattern:

  • LatencyTracker.Builder.deltaCount(int) - Maximum number of latency deltas to collect. Once this limit is reached, additional deltas are ignored.
  • LatencyTracker.Builder.bucketIntervalSize(long) - Nanosecond size of each histogram bucket. Each bucket represents measurements within this time range.
  • LatencyTracker.Builder.bucketMaximum(long) - Nanosecond threshold for the overflow bucket. Measurements at or above this value are counted in the overflow bucket.

Usage Example: 


 LatencyTracker tracker =
     (LatencyTracker.builder()).deltaCount(100000)            // Collect up to 100,000 measurements
                               .bucketIntervalSize(1_000_000) // 1 millisecond buckets
                               .bucketMaximum(100_000_000)    // Overflow bucket at 100 milliseconds
                               .build();

 tracker.startTime(Instant.now());

 // ... perform timed operations ...
 long startNano = System.nanoTime();
 // ... operation to measure ...
 long endNano = System.nanoTime();
 tracker.addDelta(endNano - startNano);

 tracker.stopTime(Instant.now());

 // Print results
 System.out.println(tracker);

Statistics Provided:

The toString() method generates a comprehensive report including:

  • Test run duration (if start and stop times are set)
  • Minimum latency
  • Median (50th percentile) latency
  • Average latency
  • 95th, 99th, 99.9th, and 99.99th percentile latencies
  • Maximum latency
  • Histogram of latencies organized by bucket intervals

Thread Safety:

This class is not thread-safe. If measurements are collected from multiple threads, external synchronization is required.

Author:
Charles W. Rapp

    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    LatencyTracker.Bucket
    Histogram bucket that tracks the count of latency measurements within a specific nanosecond time interval.
    static final class 
    LatencyTracker.Builder
    Builder for constructing LatencyTracker instances with configurable latency measurement parameters.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    INVALID_BUCKET_MAX
    Invalid maximum bucket nanosecond range results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "bucketMaximum <= 0".
    static final String
    INVALID_BUCKET_SIZE
    Invalid nanosecond bucket interval size results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "bucketIntervalSize <= 0".
    static final String
    INVALID_DELTA_COUNT
    Invalid delta deltaCount results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "count <= zero".
    static final String
    NEGATIVE_DELTA
    Invalid nanosecond latency delta results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "delta < zero".

      public String toString()
      Returns text reporting latency results based on collected deltas.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • startTime

      @Nullable public Instant startTime()
      Returns test run start time. May be null if not previously set.

      test run start time.

    • stopTime

      @Nullable public Instant stopTime()
      Returns test run stop time. May be null if not previously set.

      test run stop time.

    • deltaCount

      public int deltaCount()
      Returns number of latency deltas collected.
      Returns:
      collected deltas count.

    • bucketIntervalSize

      public long bucketIntervalSize()
      Returns bucket nanosecond interval size.
      Returns:
      bucket interval in nanoseconds.

    • maximumBucketTime

      public long maximumBucketTime()
      Returns overflow bucket's maximum nanosecond time. Any time deltas ≥ to this value are counted against the overflow bucket.
      Returns:
      overflow bucket's maximum nanosecond time.

    • minimumDelta

      public long minimumDelta()
      Returns minimum latency delta.
      Returns:
      minimum latency delta.

    • maximumDelta

      public long maximumDelta()
      Returns maximum latency delta.
      Returns:
      maximum latency delta.

    • averageDelta

      public long averageDelta()
      Returns current latency delta average.
      Returns:
      current latency delta average.

    • startTime

      public void startTime(@Nonnull Instant timestamp)
      Sets test run start time.
      Parameters:
      timestamp - test run start time.

    • stopTime

      public void stopTime(@Nonnull Instant timestamp)
      Sets test run stop time.
      Parameters:
      timestamp - test run stop time.

    • addDelta

      public int addDelta(long delta)
      Adds given latency delta to collected deltas array and updates appropriate bucket tally. Updates average, minimum, maximum delta values as needed. Ignores delta if delta collection limit previously reached.
      Parameters:
      delta - nanosecond latency delta.
      Returns:
      index into delta array.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if delta < zero.

    • builder

      public static LatencyTracker.Builder builder()
      Returns a new LatencyTracker.Builder instance.
      Returns:
      new latency tracker builder.
